title: Health-Related Social Needs (HRSN) - 211info
text: Are you an Oregon Health Plan (OHP) member facing life challenges or transitions? You may be eligible for services to help stay healthy during times of greater need. These interventions have been found to improve health outcomes and health inequities and decrease health care costs. Health-related social needs (HRSNs) are social and economic needs that affect your ability to maintain health and well-being. Who qualifies for these services? To qualify, OHP members must be in at least one of these life transitions. Additional criteria also applies for each type of HRSN service. Release from incarceration in the past 12 months Discharge from an Institution for Mental Disease (IMD) in the past 12 months Current or past involvement in the Oregon child welfare system Transitioning from Medicaid-only to dual eligibility (Medicaid and Medicare) status within the next three months or past nine months At risk of homelessness Climate Supports Payment for devices that maintain healthy temperatures and clean air, including air conditioners, heaters and air filters Mini refrigerators to keep medications cold during a power outage Portable power supplies to keep medical devices running during a power outage ​ Housing Supports Rental assistance (e.g., rental payments, utility assistance) for up to 6 months This program is not set up to meet housing emergencies (court-ordered evictions, utility disconnections) Home modifications (e.g., ramps, handrails, environmental remediation). Can also assist with temporary housing (like a hotel stay) while home modifications are being done. Tenancy support services (e.g., housing application, moving support, eviction prevention) Housing-focused navigation and/or case manager Nutrition Supports Assessments for medically-tailored meals Nutrition and cooking education Ready to apply? If you’ve determined you qualify for HRSN Benefits, follow the steps below: Gather the supporting documentation that you will need to apply Determine if you can apply though 211 or it you need to contact your Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) directly Note: 211info staff aim to return requests for assistance within 15–20 business days; however, high contact volume may cause delays. Please note that the HRSN benefits program is a multistep, multi-agency process. 211info is responsible only for the first step: eligibility screening. After screening is complete, requests are forwarded to the appropriate CCO for the next steps. Step 1: Gather Supporting Documentation Documentation needed varies by type of benefit requested. Please have the following documentation ready: For Climate Support Requests: No documentation needed For Housing Support Requests : Rental/lease agreement Court-ordered eviction notice Rent ledger or past-due notices Utility bill (electricity, natural gas, internet, water, sewer, garbage, cell phone, landline) Past-due or shut-off notice for utilities Storage unit bill Note: No documentation is needed for home modification requests For Nutrition Support Requests: Nutrition Assessment for Medically Tailored Meals completed by an RDN (registered dietitian nutritionist) or a PCP (primary care physician) to develop a nutrition care plan Note: this assessment is not required for screening, but if it is already complete, it will be submitted it alongside your enrollment application. Step 2: Determine if you can apply though 211 or if you need to contact your CCO directly If you are a member of one of the following CCOs, 211info’s Coordination Center can assist you with your application: CareOregon/Columbia Pacific/Jackson Care Connect As of July 1, 2025, the following changes will be made to the CareOregon Health Related Social Needs (HRSN) and Health Related Services Flex Programs (HRSF): For HRSN Benefit Requests – Submit an online HRSN benefits request form – For new requests only: Contact CareOregon directly at 503-416-4100 (Opt. 1 – Member, Opt. 6 – Social Needs) – 211info is continuing to focus on requests already in progress – To submit supporting documentation or PDF benefit request forms for HRSN requests, email them to: [email protected] For HRSF Benefit Requests – Submit an online HRSF benefits request form – To submit supporting documentation or PDF benefit request forms for HRSF requests, email them to: [email protected] Eastern Oregon CCO — Complete your request(s) online using this form — Or send your completed form(s) to 211info by: – Emailing to [email protected] – Faxing to 503-214-8909 (method of last resort) – Please choose either email or fax, do not send requests to both places Health Share of Oregon – Online HRSN benefits request form – For new housing and nutrition requests only : Contact CareOregon directly at 503-416-4100 (Opt. 1 – Member, Opt. 6 – Social Needs) – 211info is continuing to focus on housing requests already in progress – For new climate requests only : — HealthShare/CareOregon: Call CareOregon directly at 503-416-4100 — HealthShare/Kaiser: Call 211 — HealthShare/Legacy: Call Legacy directly at 503-415-5600 — HealthShare/OHSU: Call OHSU directly at 844-827-6572 — HealthShare/Providence: Call Providence directly at 503-574-7247 or fill out this climate device request form InterCommunity Health Network CCO — Complete your request(s) online using this form — Or send your completed form(s) to 211info by: – Emailing to [email protected] – Faxing to 503-214-8909 (method of last resort) – Please choose either email or fax, do not send requests to both places Trillium CCO — Complete your request(s) online using this form — Or send your completed form(s) to 211info by: – Emailing to [email protected] – Faxing to 503-214-8909 (method of last resort) – Please choose either email or fax, do not send requests to both places PacificSource — Complete your request(s) online using this form — Or send your completed form(s) to 211info by: – Emailing to [email protected] – Faxing to 503-214-8909 (method of last resort) – Please choose either email or fax, do not send requests to both places If you are not a member of one of the CCOs listed above Contact your CCO directly If you are not sure if you are in a CCO, call OHP Client Services: 800-273-0557 Open card (fee-for-service) members: Call 1-888-834-4304 or email [email protected] Other ways to get help For HRSN Program feedback or grievances contact: OHA Ombuds Office at 877-642-0450 or [email protected] For 211info service complaints: Call 2-1-1 and ask to speak to a supervisor. If a supervisor is not available, please leave a voicemail. If requested, a supervisor will contact you to follow-up. 211info keeps records of all requests and grievances, including the client’s name, date, nature of the grievance, and outcome.
